title: Math — Lines, Angles, and Triangles
description: SAT-style geometry problems on parallel lines, angle relationships, similarity, and triangle properties (including coordinate geometry).

questions.prompt: A line intersects two parallel lines, forming four acute angles and four obtuse angles. The measure of one of the acute angles is \((9x - 560)^\circ\). The sum of the measures of one of the acute angles and three of the obtuse angles is \((-18x + w)^\circ\). What is the value of \(w\)?
questions.explanation:
All acute angles are equal; all obtuse angles are equal and are supplementary to an acute angle.

Acute: \(9x-560\). So obtuse \(= 180-(9x-560)= -9x+740\).

Given sum: \((9x-560)+3(-9x+740)= (9x-27x)+(-560+2220)= -18x+1660\). Hence \(w=1660\).

questions.prompt: Triangle \(ABC\) is similar to triangle \(XYZ\), where \(A,B,C\) correspond to \(X,Y,Z\), respectively. In triangle \(ABC\), \(AB=170\) and \(BC=850\). In triangle \(XYZ\), \(YZ=60\). What is the length of \(XY\)?
In triangle \(ABC\), \(\angle A=54^\circ\), \(\angle B=90^\circ\), and \(\angle C=\left(\dfrac{k}{2}\right)^\circ\). What is the value of \(k\)?
questions.explanation: Triangle angles sum to \(180^\circ\): \(54+90+\frac{k}{2}=180\). So \(144+\frac{k}{2}=180\Rightarrow \frac{k}{2}=36\Rightarrow k=72\).

questions.prompt: In triangle \(ABC\), \(\angle A=50^\circ\). If triangle \(ABC\) is isosceles, which of the following is NOT a possible measure of \(\angle B\)?
questions.explanation:
Isosceles means at least two angles are equal.
- If \(B=50\), then \(C=80\): angles \(50,50,80\) (isosceles).
- If \(B=65\), then \(C=65\): angles \(50,65,65\) (isosceles).
- If \(B=80\), then \(C=50\): angles \(50,80,50\) (isosceles).
- If \(B=100\), then \(C=30\): angles \(50,100,30\) (no two equal). Not possible.

questions.prompt: Each side of equilateral triangle \(S\) is multiplied by a scale factor of \(k\) to create equilateral triangle \(T\). The length of each side of triangle \(T\) is greater than the length of each side of triangle \(S\). Which of the following could be the value of \(k\)?
questions.explanation: To make the image triangle larger, the scale factor must satisfy \(k>1\). Among the choices, only \(\tfrac{29}{28}>1\).

questions.prompt: In convex pentagon \(ABCDE\), segment \(AB\) is parallel to segment \(DE\). The measure of \(\angle B\) is \(139^\circ\) and the measure of \(\angle D\) is \(174^\circ\). What is the measure, in degrees, of \(\angle C\)?
questions.explanation:
At \(B\), the exterior angle formed by extending \(AB\) is \(180-139=41^\circ\).

At \(D\), the exterior angle adjacent to \(\angle D\) is \(180-174=6^\circ\).

Because \(AB\parallel DE\), these exterior turns add through the interior at \(C\), giving \(\angle C=41+6=47^\circ\).

questions.prompt: Triangles \(PQR\) and \(LMN\) are graphed in the xy-plane. Triangle \(PQR\) has vertices \(P(4,5),\ Q(4,7),\ R(6,5)\). Triangle \(LMN\) has vertices \(L(4,5),\ M(4,7+k),\ N(6+k,5)\), where \(k\) is a positive constant. If the measure of \(\angle Q\) is \(t^\circ\), what is the measure of \(\angle N\)?
questions.explanation:
In \(\triangle PQR\), \(PQ\) is vertical and \(PR\) is horizontal, so \(\angle P=90^\circ\). Also \(PQ=2\) and \(PR=2\), so the triangle is right isosceles and the two acute angles are each \(45^\circ\). Thus \(t=45\).

The construction for \(LMN\) preserves a right isosceles shape (legs remain perpendicular and equal), so \(\angle N=45^\circ\). Since \(90-t = 90-45=45\), the correct expression is \((90-t)^\circ\).

questions.prompt: In triangle \(JKL\), the measures of \(\angle K\) and \(\angle L\) are each \(48^\circ\). What is the measure of \(\angle J\), in degrees?
questions.explanation: Triangle angles sum to \(180^\circ\). So \(\angle J = 180-48-48 = 84\).

questions.prompt: In right triangle \(ABC\), \(\angle C\) is the right angle and \(BC=162\). Point \(D\) on side \(AB\) is connected to point \(E\) on side \(AC\) such that \(DE\) is parallel to \(BC\) and \(CE = 2AE\). What is the length of \(DE\)?
questions.explanation:
Since \(DE\parallel BC\), triangles \(ADE\) and \(ABC\) are similar.

Given \(CE=2AE\), then \(AC = AE+CE = AE+2AE = 3AE\), so \(\dfrac{AE}{AC}=\dfrac{1}{3}\).

By similarity, \(\dfrac{DE}{BC}=\dfrac{AE}{AC}=\dfrac{1}{3}\), so \(DE=\frac{1}{3}\cdot 162=54\).

questions.prompt: In triangle \(XYZ\), angle \(Y\) is a right angle. Point \(P\) lies on \(XZ\), and point \(Q\) lies on \(YZ\) such that \(PQ\) is parallel to \(XY\). If the measure of angle \(XZY\) is \(63^\circ\), what is the measure, in degrees, of angle \(XPQ\)?
questions.explanation:
Since \(PQ\parallel XY\), angle \(ZPQ\) equals angle \(\angle Y\) in the small similar triangle, but more directly: \(XY\perp YZ\) because \(\angle Y=90^\circ\), so \(PQ\perp YZ\) as well.

At vertex \(Z\), \(\angle XZY=63^\circ\) is the angle between \(ZX\) and \(ZY\). Because \(PQ\perp ZY\), the angle between \(PQ\) and \(ZX\) is \(90-63=27^\circ\). That acute angle is \(\angle ZPX\)'s corresponding angle at \(P\), namely \(\angle ZPQ=27^\circ\).

Points \(X\), \(P\), \(Z\) are collinear, so \(\angle XPQ\) is supplementary to \(\angle ZPQ\): \(\angle XPQ = 180-27=153^\circ\).
